title: Restyle create/add affordances as accent text-links (Zeroheight style)
description: Prompted by the user comparing the running CMS (`referencia-3.png`) with Zeroheight (`referencia.png`): SystemBook's structure-growing actions read wrong. In Zeroheight, 'Add subcategory', 'Add page' and 'Add tab' are quiet blue text-links with a '+' icon â€” the single, obvious 'grow your structure' affordance. In SystemBook the equivalent actions ('Nova seĂ§ĂŁo', 'Adicionar pĂˇgina', '+ Aba', the header '+ Adicionar menu') are `text-muted-foreground` gray text (current code) and rendered as gray filled boxes in an older build (`referencia-3.png`) â€” neither reads as an intentional action. Per `plano-de-interface.md` ('azul como Ăşnico acento de aĂ§ĂŁo'), promote these create affordances to accent-colored text-links: blue is the sole action accent, and creating structure IS an action, so this is consistent rather than dilutive. This does NOT touch the active/selected nav tint or the primary 'Publicar' button.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Nova seĂ§ĂŁo' and 'Adicionar pĂˇgina' (`SidebarTree.tsx` â€” `InlineCreate` and `CreatePageForm` collapsed triggers) render as accent-colored (`text-primary`) text-links with a leading '+' icon, no persistent gray/filled background, matching Zeroheight's 'Add subcategory'/'Add page' treatment
'+ Aba' (`PageContentPage.tsx` â€” `AddTab`, both in the tab bar and the standalone zero-tabs case) uses the same accent text-link treatment
The header '+ Adicionar menu' (`AppHeader.tsx` â€” `AddMenu` collapsed trigger) uses the same accent treatment, staying visually consistent with the other create affordances
Hover/focus give a subtle reinforcement (e.g. slightly darker accent) without introducing a filled box; keyboard focus stays clearly visible
The expanded inline-create forms (input + confirm/cancel) are unchanged in behavior; only the collapsed trigger's resting style changes

No regression to the active-nav tint (selected page / active menu pill keep their subtle `bg-accent`) or to the primary 'Publicar' action â€” blue stays reserved for actions, and these create-links are the only additions

steps.description: Define the shared accent text-link style
steps.details: Establish a single className (or tiny helper) for a '+ label' accent text-link: `text-primary`, `hover:text-primary` darker, `inline-flex items-center gap-1.5`, small text, no background at rest. Reuse it across all four call sites so the affordance is uniform.

steps.description: Apply to the sidebar create actions
steps.details: Swap the `text-muted-foreground` collapsed triggers in `SidebarTree.tsx` (`InlineCreate` for 'Nova seĂ§ĂŁo', `CreatePageForm` for 'Adicionar pĂˇgina') to the accent text-link. Keep the expand-to-input behavior intact.

steps.description: Apply to the editor '+ Aba' and header '+ Adicionar menu'
steps.details: Update `AddTab` in `PageContentPage.tsx` (tab bar + standalone) and `AddMenu` in `AppHeader.tsx` to the same accent text-link. Confirm the header '+' still reads well next to the menu pills.

steps.description: Verify in the browser against the reference
steps.details: Rebuild the admin, load the shell, and confirm all four create affordances render as blue text-links with no gray fill, matching `referencia.png`. Confirm the active-nav tint and 'Publicar' are unchanged. Screenshot the sidebar + header + editor tab bar.

technicalNotes: `referencia-3.png` shows these as gray filled boxes; the current working-tree code already made them ghost gray text (`text-muted-foreground`). This task moves them the rest of the way to Zeroheight's accent text-link â€” the gray-box appearance in the screenshot is a stale build, so step 4 should rebuild before judging.
Keep blue strictly for actions: do not accent the section-header category labels, breadcrumb segments, or non-action nav â€” only the create affordances.
`text-primary` is the admin accent token from TASK-82; reuse it rather than a hard-coded blue.

Coordinate with TASK-95 (which flattens the remaining chrome fills) â€” both touch `SidebarTree.tsx` and `AppHeader.tsx`, so land this first and let TASK-95 build on top to avoid conflicting rewrites.
